ogTitle: Marmor vs Maroochy River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Marmor and Maroochy River.
heading: Marmor vs Maroochy River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Marmor, Queensland and Maroochy River, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Maroochy River has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Marmor (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Marmor or Maroochy River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Marmor has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Maroochy River scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Marmor scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Maroochy River scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Marmor or Maroochy River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Maroochy River has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
